metadata.title: Acme Health Patient Intake API v1

components.title: acme-health-intake-api
components.description: AWS serverless patient intake API: API Gateway (HTTP) → Lambda (Python 3.12) → DynamoDB + S3. Deployed via Terraform with GRC controls layered on top of the cgep-app-starter workload.
components.purpose: Receive and store patient intake submissions in a HIPAA-aligned AWS environment with KMS encryption, TLS enforcement, audit logging, resilience controls, and least-privilege IAM.

control-implementations.description: CMMC Level 2 / NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2 controls implemented by the Acme Health intake API Terraform configuration and OPA policy suite.

implemented-requirements.description: PHI at rest is encrypted with a customer-managed KMS key (aws_kms_key.phi) satisfying FIPS-validated cryptography requirements. The S3 uploads bucket uses SSE-KMS via aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration. The DynamoDB intake table uses server_side_encryption with the same CMK. Lambda environment variables reference the KMS key ARN and the function has kms:Decrypt/Encrypt/GenerateDataKey permissions. The Rego policies s3_kms.rego and dynamodb_kms.rego fail the Terraform plan if either data store deviates from CMK encryption. Addresses GAP-01 and GAP-02.

CUI in transit is protected by TLS. The S3 uploads bucket has a bucket policy (aws_s3_bucket_policy.uploads_tls) with a Deny on aws:SecureTransport=false covering all s3:* actions on both the bucket and its objects. API Gateway exposes only HTTPS endpoints. The Rego policy s3_tls.rego fails any plan where the uploads bucket policy omits the SecureTransport deny. Addresses GAP-03.

S3 versioning is enabled on the uploads bucket (aws_s3_bucket_versioning.uploads, status=Enabled), ensuring CUI objects can be recovered after accidental overwrites or deletes, satisfying media backup and sanitization requirements. The Rego policy s3_versioning.rego fails any plan where versioning is absent or disabled. Addresses GAP-04.

Least-privilege IAM is enforced at two layers. The Lambda execution role (aws_iam_role_policy.lambda_inline) grants only dynamodb:GetItem/PutItem/UpdateItem on the intake table and s3:GetObject/PutObject on the uploads bucket — not wildcard actions. The Rego policy iam_least_privilege.rego fails any plan where an IAM role policy contains s3:* or dynamodb:* wildcard actions. Addresses GAP-07.

Lambda resilience and monitoring controls reduce the blast radius of function failures and support security event detection. A dead-letter queue (aws_sqs_queue.lambda_dlq) captures failed invocations via the dead_letter_config block. AWS X-Ray active tracing is enabled (tracing_config mode=Active) for distributed tracing and anomaly detection. The Lambda IAM role has sqs:SendMessage on the DLQ ARN. Addresses GAP-06.

API Gateway access logging is enabled on the $default stage (aws_apigatewayv2_stage.default) with destination CloudWatch log group /aws/apigateway/acme-health-intake (90-day retention). The JSON log format captures requestId, sourceIp, requestTime, routeKey, HTTP status, and responseLength — creating audit records sufficient to reconstruct user activity and system events. Addresses GAP-08.

API Gateway throttling (burst limit 100, rate limit 50 req/s) enforces a boundary protection control, protecting the Lambda backend from volumetric denial-of-service and unauthorized traffic. The Lambda function does not set reserved_concurrent_executions because the sandbox account's total concurrency is at the AWS floor of 10, but throttling at the API layer provides the primary boundary enforcement. Addresses GAP-08.
